expectations.evidence: File packages--nextjs--middleware.ts exists in the outputs directory with full x402 payment proxy middleware implementation (62 lines).

expectations.text: Uses x402 v2 API (paymentProxy, x402ResourceServer, HTTPFacilitatorClient)
expectations.evidence: middleware.ts imports: `import { paymentProxy } from "@x402/next";`, `import { HTTPFacilitatorClient, x402ResourceServer } from "@x402/core/server";`. All three v2 API constructs are used: paymentProxy wraps routes, HTTPFacilitatorClient is instantiated with url, x402ResourceServer is created with facilitatorClient.
middleware.ts line 3: `import { registerExactEvmScheme } from "@x402/evm/exact/server";` and line 14: `registerExactEvmScheme(server);`

expectations.text: Uses CAIP-2 network format (eip155:84532) not legacy names
expectations.evidence: .env.development line 11: `NETWORK=eip155:84532` with comment explaining CAIP-2 format. middleware.ts line 9: `const network = process.env.NETWORK as \`${string}:${string}\``;` which enforces CAIP-2 colon-separated format.

middleware.ts lines 4-5: `import { createPaywall } from "@x402/paywall"; import { evmPaywall } from "@x402/paywall/evm";` and lines 17-24: `const paywall = createPaywall().withNetwork(evmPaywall).withConfig({...}).build();`

expectations.text: A protected API route handler exists
expectations.evidence: File packages--nextjs--app--api--payment--builder--route.ts exists with a GET handler that returns builder JSON data. Comment states: "This route is protected by x402 middleware. Clients must include a valid X-PAYMENT header."

expectations.text: Environment variables for facilitator, wallet, network configured
x402 packages added to nextjs package.json

expectations.evidence: middleware.ts lines 59-61: `export const config = { matcher: ["/api/payment/:path*", "/payment/:path*"] };` which covers both the API routes and page routes defined in the paymentProxy config.
scaffold.config.ts line 17: `targetNetworks: [chains.baseSepolia]` with pollingInterval set to 3000 (appropriate for L2).

eval_feedback.overall: Excellent implementation. All 10 expectations fully met. The output demonstrates proper use of x402 v2 API with all required components: paymentProxy, HTTPFacilitatorClient, x402ResourceServer, registerExactEvmScheme, createPaywall with evmPaywall, CAIP-2 network format, protected API route, environment variables, x402 package dependencies, middleware matcher, and baseSepolia target network. The implementation also includes thoughtful extras like a frontend demo page, CLI test script, and Header navigation link.
